notes: Uses cost-aware Neyman-style allocation when treatment and control costs differ.
Reported MDE reflects the current budget when budget is supplied, otherwise it matches the requested effect size.
Assumes a paired design where each subject is observed before and after treatment.
Effect size is interpreted as the standardized mean difference in paired outcomes.
Uses cost-aware Neyman-style allocation when treatment and control costs differ.
Reported MDE reflects the current budget when budget is supplied, otherwise it matches the requested effect size.
Clustered design inflates the individual-level sample size by a design effect of 1.950.
Requires about 18 treatment clusters and 18 control clusters.

analysis_plan.estimand: Average treatment effect on the outcome mean
analysis_plan.adjustment_recommendation: Use CUPED or ANCOVA adjustment if high-quality pre-period covariates are available.
analysis_plan.randomization_recommendation: Use blocked randomization on pre-period risk or geography to stabilize balance.
notes: Pre-register the estimand, alpha, minimum detectable effect, and stopping rule.
Monitor sample ratio mismatch and attrition before locking the primary analysis.
